Player 1 Ready 🕹️ Career Strategy 🗺️ Unlock Your Potential 🔓

Choose Your
Career Shape

Before AI chooses it for you.

PROLOGUE 📂
Top Secret
"What do you do?" is the wrong question.
The right question is: "What shape are you?"
1

In the 1980s, McKinsey had a problem. They were hiring brilliant specialists — people who knew everything about one thing — and watching them fail spectacularly when projects needed breadth, judgment, and cross-functional collaboration. So they invented a term internally: the "T-shaped person." Someone with real depth in one area, but enough breadth to play well with others.

2

The concept leaked out. IDEO's Tim Brown made it famous. Agile teams adopted it. And then — slowly, quietly — people started realising the T was just the beginning. There were more shapes. A lot more.

3

This is the complete guide to all of them. And why — right now, in the AI Era — understanding your shape isn't optional anymore.

Level 1

The Great Disruption 🤖

Let's be blunt about what's happening globally.

📉
300M
Full-time jobs exposed to automation
(Goldman Sachs)
🔄
44%
Of core worker skills expected to disrupt
(WEF)
🚀
97M
New AI-augmented roles emerging globally
(WEF)
⚡
65%
Of tasks have high automation potential
(McKinsey)
📈
10x
Productivity boost for cross-domain AI
(Harvard/BCG)
⚠️
85M
Routine jobs displaced globally
(WEF)
Level 2

The Specialist Killer ⚔️

VS
The Specialist Killer

AI is a specialist-killing machine. It can pass the bar exam. Diagnose skin cancer better than dermatologists. Write code, generate designs, summarise legal documents. Every pure I-shaped knowledge worker is staring down the barrel of a technology that does their one deep thing — fast, cheap, tirelessly.

The Human Shield

But here's what AI cannot do: synthesise across messy human domains, exercise judgment under ambiguity, build trust across a table, and lead people. Those are T, Pi, E, M, Comb, and X-shaped skills. The shapes that know enough about enough to know what questions to ask the AI.

"AI doesn't replace T-shaped people. It makes them 10x more powerful — because now they have an I-shaped assistant for every domain they touch."

Level 3

AI Survival Index 🛡️

Higher = More Resilient. Depth alone no longer protects you.

Level 4

The Skill Archetypes 🧬

These aren't personality types. They're not fixed. They're the shape your skills make right now — and the shape you're aiming to grow into.

18% AI Survival

I-Shaped

The Deep Diver

One skill. Drilled all the way down.

Picture a neurosurgeon at a dinner party. Someone asks what they do. They explain — in loving detail — the specific sub-type of aneurysm they operated on this morning. The table goes quiet. This is the I-shaped person in their natural habitat: incomprehensibly deep, completely unaware that nobody else is following. Examples range from sushi master Jiro Ono to brilliant academic researchers like Marie Curie.

The I-shape is the oldest career shape in human history. One thing, done to perfection, for life. And for centuries, that was enough. Then AI arrived and learned to do their one thing. In seconds. For free.

Punchline

They can spend three hours explaining the exact chemical makeup of the iceberg, but they won't notice the ship is sinking.

Surgeons Tax Lawyers Academic Researchers
72% AI Survival

T-Shaped

The Versatile Collaborator

One deep expertise. Enough breadth to be dangerous.

The T-shaped person is the MVP (Most Valuable Professional) that every startup founder describes when they say "we need someone who can wear many hats." They mean: someone with a genuine skill, who also doesn't stare blankly when you say "customer", "revenue", or "please help us with this email."

The horizontal bar of the T isn't fluff. It's the most underrated professional skill there is: the ability to collaborate without being a specialist. To sit in a room full of engineers and ask the right question. To sit with designers and not break things. McKinsey invented this shape. Tim Brown from IDEO made it famous.

Punchline

The T-shaped chef knows every cuisine but is a world-class expert in pasta. He will absolutely judge your mac & cheese — but he'll do it with empathy and a wine pairing suggestion.

Product Managers UX Designers Agile Leaders
84% AI Survival

π-Shaped

The Bridge Builder

Two deep pillars. Where real magic happens.

Here's the thing nobody tells you about having two deep skillsets: the value isn't additive. It's multiplicative. A lawyer who codes isn't worth a lawyer plus a developer. They're worth 3x either — because they can see the entire system that everyone else only sees half of.

Steve Jobs was famously π-shaped before it had a name — possessing deep pillars in calligraphy/design and computer technology. Brian May is a legendary guitarist and an astrophysicist. The intersection is where careers become irreplaceable.

Punchline

A π-shaped person applies for a job posting. They meet 100% of the requirements — and then 80% of another job posting they also spotted. They apply to both. They get both offers.

Full-Stack Devs Data Engineers Creative Technologists
88% AI Survival

M-Shaped

The Overachiever

Three deep pillars. Pi's intimidating sibling.

The M-shaped person is someone who kept going. They have three distinct domains of genuine mastery. This usually takes a decade or more. Christopher Nolan writes, directs, and produces. Think of India's Dr. Homi Bhabha—a world-class physicist, a visionary institution builder, and an international diplomat.

The danger of M is maintaining three pillars simultaneously. Real expertise demands continuous feeding. Three of those at once is genuinely hard. But the ones who pull it off? They sit at the intersection of three worlds nobody else even knows are connected.

Punchline

An M-shaped person walks into a meeting about a medical product's legal liability and software architecture. Everyone else brought one specialist. They brought themselves. The meeting is 40 minutes shorter than planned.

CTOs Lead Architects Innovation Directors
82% AI Survival

E-Shaped

The Executor Engine

Four horizontal layers. One deep trunk.

The E-shape is a different kind of shape — not more pillars of depth, but four distinct horizontal capabilities growing from one vertical trunk. The four E's: Expertise (deep craft), Experience (track record), Execution (ability to ship), and Exploration (curiosity).

Linus Torvalds isn't just an expert coder; he built Linux through relentless execution and community management. Most people have Expertise. Fewer have Experience. Very few have real Execution — the kind where they've actually shipped something significant, led a team through uncertainty, and lived with the consequences.

Punchline

An E-shaped PM is in a meeting where someone says "we should really explore whether this is viable." The E-shaped PM says "I built a prototype last week. Here are the user test results." The meeting ends. A product ships.

VPs of Engineering Principal Engineers Operations Leads
94% AI Survival

Comb-Shaped

The Legendary Unicorn

Many deep pillars. Vast breadth.

The Comb is what happens when a T-shaped person keeps growing for twenty years and never stops. It's not a beginner's shape. It's the accumulated result of genuine curiosity applied relentlessly over decades. Leonardo da Vinci was Comb-shaped. Rabindranath Tagore was a poet, philosopher, painter, and educationalist.

The comb's weakness is recruiters. LinkedIn is built for I-shaped and T-shaped people. A comb's CV reads like a fever dream — artist, engineer, investor, author, all somehow the same person.

Punchline

A comb-shaped person sends their CV. The recruiter reads it. Calls their manager. The manager calls their manager. Three days later, someone creates a new job title specifically for this person. They accept. They immediately start changing things.

Serial Founders Chief Strategy Officers Polymaths
96% AI Survival

X-Shaped

The Leader

Craft meets leadership. Strategy meets execution.

The X is not a shape of knowledge — it's a shape of influence. The two axes are craft depth and leadership depth. Both have to be real. A leader who can't do the work loses the engineers. An engineer who can't lead loses the org. The X-shaped person sits at the cross-section.

Satya Nadella is X-shaped. He came up as a deeply technical engineer—but rebuilt Microsoft not by writing code, but by rewriting its culture. Ratan Tata similarly combined deep industrial knowledge with visionary, empathetic leadership. Both axes, at the same time.

Punchline

An X-shaped leader walks into a standup. The engineers respect them because they spotted the architectural flaw before anyone else. The board respects them because they turned that flaw into a strategic pivot. The intern is taking notes so fast their hand is cramping.

CEOs & Founders CTOs Directors of Product
Level 5

Evolution Map 🗺️

Careers are no longer static titles. They are evolving geometries. Follow the path to unlock the next level of AI immunity.

⛏️
I-Shape
🛠️
T-Shape
⚖️
π / E Shape
🔮
M-Shape
👑
🚀
X / Comb
Level 6

Cheat Sheet 🎒

Tap or hover cards to flip and review the roster.

I

Specialist

Tap to flip ↺

Most vulnerable. AI does your one thing. Urgently need to add a horizontal bar.

Fix: Collaborate

T

Connector

Tap to flip ↺

Solid foundation. Breadth means you can use AI across domains. The startup MVP.

Fix: Add 2nd Pillar

π

Dual Master

Tap to flip ↺

Very strong. Two pillars means two AI amplifiers. The synthesis is fully human.

Fix: Execute

E

Expander

Tap to flip ↺

Execution + Exploration. You get things built and shipped in the real world.

Fix: Build 3rd Pillar

M

Triple Master

Tap to flip ↺

Excellent. Three domains is three angles. Highly rare, hard to maintain.

Fix: Avoid Rust

COMB

Unicorn

Tap to flip ↺

Maximum resilience. Every tooth gets an AI amplifier. You're a one-person lab.

Goal: Orchestrate

X

The Leader

Tap to flip ↺

Near-immune. Leadership, judgment, and trust-building across humans is the last frontier AI cannot touch.

Status: Apex
Level 7

Self-Discovery 🔍

One question. Honest answer. If your job disappeared tomorrow, how would you describe what you bring to the next one?

You Are

✨

Your personalized share text:

"I just discovered my career skill shape is ! 🚀

The world doesn't just need specialists anymore. What's your shape? Find out here:"
https://hariprav.in/skill-shapes

Note: LinkedIn & FB block pre-filled text. We will copy it for you to paste!

Level 8: The Final Truth

The uncomfortable truth is this: most people have never thought about their shape.

They've thought about their job title. Their salary band. Their industry. But not the actual geometry of their knowledge — how deep it goes, how wide it spreads, how many pillars it rests on.

And in a world where AI is rapidly doing the single-pillar work, the geometry is everything. Not because shapes are a magic career formula, but because they force you to ask the right question: am I building depth, breadth, or both? Am I growing the horizontal bar, or just drilling deeper in the same hole?

"The best shapes aren't born.
They're built — one deliberate expansion at a time."

You don't have to be a comb by next Tuesday. But you should probably know what shape you are today — and have a rough idea of which direction you're growing in.

Keep Learning, Keep Adapting 🧠

Curated & Designed by Hari Pravin 🎨
